stem:
(10x + 35)/5 − q/9 = c(x − 6)

In the given equation, q and c are constants, and q > 0. If the equation has infinitely many solutions, what is the value of q?
rationale: Simplify the left side: (10x + 35)/5 = 2x + 7. The equation becomes 2x + 7 − q/9 = cx − 6c. For infinitely many solutions, the coefficients of x and the constant terms must match. From x: 2 = c. From constants: 7 − q/9 = −6c = −12, so q/9 = 19 and q = 171.
